text:
Danjin (ダンジン, Danjin) is one of 4 th Prince Tserriednich's bodyguards. He is one of the five personal guards that Tserriednich has successfully become a Provisional Hunter to bypass the normal fifteen employee limit, allowing Tserriednich to have twenty employees journey with him to the Dark Continent. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 374 

Danjin is one of the two representatives Tserriednich sent to attend Kurapika's first round of Nen classes. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 369 

Appearance

Danjin has dark hair and thick lips. His head is shaven except for a cylindrical tuft at the top. He styles his beard into mutton chops, extending to below his lips and curving sharply toward them. As a Royal Bodyguard, he wears a black suit and a tie. 

Personality

Danjin is a pragmatic and accommodating man. He appears to dislike Myuhan's violent and impulsive personality, often reigning him in and going so far as to apologize in his place. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 370 

Plot

Succession Contest arc

Danjin is present when Theta informs 4 th Prince Tserriednich that all of his personal bodyguards have passed the 289 th Hunter Exam. 

At night on the first day, Tserriednich agrees to send his bodyguards to learn Nen under Kurapika. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 368 The next morning, Danjin and Myuhan are sent by him with permission to "kill them all" if they failed to learn Nen within two weeks. Danjin picks up on Myuhan's interest in the matter and warns him not to fail to learn Nen on purpose. 

During Kurapika's explanation of the rules and expectations for the Nen classes, he points out a white line that he requests the participants not to cross. Myuhan intentionally steps on the line and pretends not to notice to test Kurapika. Kurapika asks him for his name and post, and Danjin gently punches Myuhan to get him to answer Kurapika and not pretend not to have heard him. Danjin also restrains Myuhan after he is provoked by Kurapika's retort, and assures Kurapika there will be no more disturbances from them. A while later, he witnesses Barrigen being killed by a small pack of snakes. He contacts Tserriednich to inform him of the events but is ordered to continue. When instructed to put his hands together by Kurapika, Danjin clasps his hands together Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 371 and joins the prayer type of Nen training, until the end of the training session. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 372 

The following day, Danjin returns to Room 1014 for Kurapika's lecture. Myuhan dies in a similar manner to Barrigen, yet Danjin does not intervene when Maor and Satobi accuse Kurapika of being behind the attacks. While reporting to Tserriednich, he speculates the culprit to be a Nen user and one of the 21 people in Room 1014. When Tserriednich asks for his opinion, Danjin replies he intends to continue going to the lectures and that he believes Kurapika to be innocent, as the Hunters would benefit from creating a situation of stasis. Tserriednich allows him to continue on condition he brings a "gift" to prove that he is not being controlled by a Nen user by the last lecture. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 376 

Danjin is seen on the seventh day, participating in Kurapika's Nen classes. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 382 

On the ninth day, the participants begin to question repeating the same training, wondering if they will really be able to use Nen. Danjin and his fellow participants observe Kurapika's demonstration of Water Divination. When Kurapika announces that he and Bill would privately watch over the participants as they discovered their Nen type in another room, Danjin points out that if he gives them a chart with the results, they could perform the test without the need for supervision. Kurapika justifies his decision with the training requirements, the risk he took by showing his own Nen type, and his camp's wish for a stalemate. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 386 The participants reluctantly let Kurapika continue, and gather to discuss possible courses of action to take if Kurapika's actions were determined to be nefarious as they wait to judge Ladiolus, the first participant to have her Nen type divined, when she comes out. Benjamin's soldiers propose to check for signs of manipulation themselves and arrest Kurapika and Bill if signs of manipulation are present. The participants accept this, and Danjin contacts Room 1004 to report this and reconfirm Tserriednich's orders. Danjin is in the midst of contacting his superiors when Ladiolus steps out and is confirmed by Furykov to not be manipulated. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 388 

On the tenth day, Danjin turns up at Room 1014 for Kurapika's Nen lesson. Kurapika and Bill invite Longhi into Room 1014's master bedroom to have her Nen divined, but takes an unnaturally long amount of time, prompting Sakata to knock and ask if anything is wrong. Kurapika steps out and says that negotiations are taking a while because they and Longhi are unable to agree on the terms of the truce, but promises to clear up any doubts the participants have later. Kurapika steps back in, and a while later returns to Room 1014's living room together with Longhi. Kurapika apologizes and explains that they are still unable to agree on the details, and invites Tenftory in to have his Nen divined. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 401 Later, Tenftory reports to 3 rd Prince Zhang Lei that all of the students who have taken Kurapika's Nen training, are able to utilize Nen. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 389 

Abilities & Powers

As a Provisional Hunter, Danjin has all the benefits granted by his status as a Hunter. However, these are all temporary, and his Hunter status and license would expire after the voyage. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 358 Tserriednich believed him and Myuhan to be able to kill everyone studying Nen under Kurapika. 

Nen

Danjin had his Aura Nodes fully opened by Kurapika's Stealth Dolphin. As of Day 10, he should currently be in the process of learning Ten to contain the leaking aura. 

Former Nen Abilities

Manga Appearances

Trivia

For the purposes of Kurapika's Nen training, Danjin is a "prayer". 
His name and appearance may be based on Djinn, a genie from Arabian folklore.
